children.heading: 44, .—TitleUnited States Code
children.label: a 44, .—TitleUnited States Code
children.text: This subchapter does not diminish the authority under section 3510 of the Director to direct, and of an agency to make, disclosures that are not inconsistent with any applicable law.

children.heading: 13 44, .—Titleand TitleUnited States Code
children.label: b 13 44, .—Titleand TitleUnited States Code
children.text: This subchapter does not diminish the authority of the Bureau of the Census to provide information in accordance with sections 8, 16, 301, and 401 of title 13 and .section 2108 of this title

children.heading: 13, .—TitleUnited States Code
children.label: c 13, .—TitleUnited States Code
children.text: This subchapter shall not be construed as authorizing the disclosure for nonstatistical purposes of demographic data or information collected by the Bureau of the Census pursuant to .section 9 of title 13
section 12, 20, or 59 of the Federal Energy Administration Act of 1974 (, 779, 790h);15 U.S.C. 771
section 11 of the Energy Supply and Environmental Coordination Act of 1974 (); or15 U.S.C. 796
section 205 or 407 of the Department of Energy Organization Act (, 7177).42 U.S.C. 7135

children.heading: 201 1974.—Sectionof Congressional Budget Act of
children.label: e 201 1974.—Sectionof Congressional Budget Act of
children.text: This subchapter shall not be construed to limit any authorities of the Congressional Budget Office to work (consistent with laws governing the confidentiality of information the disclosure of which would be a violation of law) with databases of Designated Statistical Agencies (as defined in section 3576(e)), either separately or, for data that may be shared pursuant to section 3576(c) or other authority, jointly in order to improve the general utility of these databases for the statistical purpose of analyzing pension and health care financing issues.

children.heading: .—Preemption of State Law
children.label: f .—Preemption of State Law
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall preempt applicable State law regarding the confidentiality of data collected by the States.

children.heading: .—Statutes Regarding False Statements
children.label: g .—Statutes Regarding False Statements
children.text: Notwithstanding section 3572, information collected by an agency for exclusively statistical purposes under a pledge of confidentiality may be provided by the collecting agency to a law enforcement agency for the prosecution of submissions to the collecting agency of false statistical information under statutes that authorize criminal penalties (such as ) or civil penalties for the provision of false statistical information, unless such disclosure or use would otherwise be prohibited under Federal law.section 221 of title 13

children.heading: .—Construction
children.label: h .—Construction
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed as restricting or diminishing any confidentiality protections or penalties for unauthorized disclosure that otherwise apply to data or information collected for statistical purposes or nonstatistical purposes, including, but not limited to, section 6103 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.

children.heading: .—Authority of Congress
children.label: i .—Authority of Congress
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed to affect the authority of the Congress, including its committees, members, or agents, to obtain data or information for a statistical purpose, including for oversight of an agency’s statistical activities.
